content: Just as the linguistic concept of reflexion (reflection) taught in high school writing asks students to revisit their own drafted arguments to spot inconsistencies and align meaning with intent, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow the structured reflective writing workshop framework to improve multi-perspective self-correction. Specifically, LLMs can be guided through the same incremental reflection steps students use to revise essays: first check for logical inconsistencies across generated steps, then revise outputs to align with the core problem goal, mirroring how student writers refine work through guided metacognition.
